found.quote: General representations and warranties survive for 12 months after closing, and fundamental representations expire at the same time.
Seller will use commercially reasonable efforts after closing to request counterparty consents for Material Contracts, but receipt of those consents is not a condition to Buyer's obligation to close.
Buyer's closing statement will be final and binding when delivered, with no separate objection, negotiation, or accountant review process.
Buyer's obligation to close is conditioned on Seller's representations being true in all material respects, Seller's covenant compliance, receipt of required consents, and delivery of lien releases.
